title: Systems and methods for virtual and augmented reality
abstract: A computer implemented method of facilitating communication between first and second users includes displaying, by a first head-worn device, a first virtual object to the first user first user wearing the first head-worn device. The method also includes displaying, by a second head-worn device, a second virtual object to the second user wearing the second head-worn device. The method further includes facilitating, by the first and second head-worn devices, communications between the first and second users using the first and second virtual objects to simulate the first and second users being present in a common environment.
claims: 1. A system for sharing a virtual experience involving a real-world object and a virtual object, comprising: a first head-worn viewing component including a first sensor; a first hand-held controller component operatively coupled to the first head-worn viewing component; a first computing component operatively coupled to the first head-worn viewing component and the first hand-held controller component; a second head-worn viewing component including a second sensor; a second hand-held controller component operatively coupled to the second head-worn viewing component; and a second computing component operatively coupled to the second head-worn viewing component and the second hand-held controller component; wherein the first sensor is configured to capture an image of a real-world object from a first user's view of the real-world object, wherein the first computing component is configured to generate a view of a virtual object corresponding to the real-world object from the first user's view of the real-world object, based on the captured image; and wherein the second head-worn viewing component is configured to display the view of the virtual object from the first user's view of the real-world object to a second user wearing the second head-worn viewing component; and wherein the view of the virtual object from the first user's view of the real-world object is displayed on the second head worn device in real-time with the capturing the image of a real-world object from the first user's view of the real-world object.
